title: SOMA Pilipinas at the Heart of Downtown Recovery with Explosion of New Public Art, Restaurants and Jeepney Tour — SOMA PILIPINAS
content: # SOMA Pilipinas at the Heart of Downtown Recovery with Explosion of New Public Art, Restaurants and Jeepney Tour. The "Mahal Jeepney" is a 1948 model Willy restored and owned by local superstar musician Tori Y Moi, who donated it to SOMA Pilipinas last year and is now a cultural asset for the district. SOMA Pilipinas, the city and state recognized Filipino Cultural Heritage District proves to be a leader in revitalizing downtown San Francisco by creating a new narrative and cultural boom through innovative economic recovery strategies rooted in equity, community, collaboration and cultural arts. > “Operating in downtown San Francisco has been a challenge but we’re excited about all the new art and cultural activities in SOMA Pilipinas, and being a  part of a rich ecosystem of Filipino arts, cuisine, and entrepreneurs.”  shared Manuel Ramirez, owner of Bonchon and Izzy and Wooks, the first restaurant to open in the Metreon since the pandemic.

title: SOMA PILIPINAS
content: This year marks ten years of SOMA Pilipinas as San Francisco’s Filipino Cultural Heritage District. The SOMA Pilipinas Filipino Heritage District is a celebration of the love, pride and people power of generations of Filipinos in San Francisco and beyond. San Francisco’s Filipino Cultural Heritage District. Over the past decade, SOMA Pilipinas has grown through the collective work of community members committed to preserving Filipino presence, culture, and history in South of Market while continuing to respond to the ongoing challenges facing the neighborhood today. **10 Years of SOMA Pilipinas**. As SOMA Pilipinas enters its next decade, we invite our community and allies to help sustain this work through donations and continued support for preserving Filipino history and presence in South of Market. As displacement and funding cuts continue to threaten our communities, become 1 of 100 monthly sustainers helping power the next decade of SOMA Pilipinas. Join us for gatherings including Filipino Heritage Night with the Golden State Valkyries in August, the Dekada Dance Party in September,.

title: Discover SF's Filipino Cultural Heritage District - NBC Los Angeles
content: California Live's Jobeth Devera tours SOMA Pilipinas, San Francisco's Filipino Cultural Heritage District, first recognized by the city in 2016.
